rationale.R1.2: A general request to look at a research paragraph centrally includes checking whether the motivation, method, results, and contribution claim cohere logically.
rationale.R1.3: Because the paragraph reports experiments and quantitative outcomes, the review responsibility includes checking whether the concluding claims match the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.6: The artifact is a paragraph, so sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision are central expected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this broad delegation must preserve uncertainty about the user’s intended depth and review criteria.
rationale.RX.2: The response should avoid projecting responsibilities such as novelty audit or experimental-design review beyond what the request supports.
rationale.RX.3: The delegation is only to look at the paragraph, so responsibilities must remain bounded to paragraph-level research-writing review.
rationale.RX.4: Because the paragraph concerns real robotic manipulation claims, the projected review should avoid enabling misleading or unsafe downstream claims.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the wording of the delegation and the specific claims present in the artifact.
